page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m1.2xlarge is a burstable instance type belonging to the ecs.t5 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. It features 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reflect the burstable nature and resource constraints of the hardware. Single-core performance is limited, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the weak and poor categories respectively. However, multi-core performance is more robust, with the stress-ng multi-core test and Passmark CPU score achieving average ratings. Memory bandwidth is stable, showing average performance in both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached writes. Cryptographic and vector operations are supported by hardware flags such as AVX, AVX-512F, and AES, which is reflected in average Passmark CPU extended instructions and encryption scores.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.t5-c1m1.2xlarge is its low memory allocation relative to its vCPU count, combined with its burstable CPU allocation. This makes it highly cost-efficient for workloads that experience intermittent CPU spikes rather than sustained high utilization. It is not suitable for memory-intensive databases or heavy single-threaded processing due to poor single-core benchmark results. Appropriate workloads include lightweight multi-threaded applications, basic web serving, software compilation tasks, and development or testing environments that require multiple vCPUs but minimal memory overhead.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m1.2xlarge is an x86_64 burstable instance within the ecs.t5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 8.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a low 1:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on a KVM hypervisor and offering a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates weak single-core performance but average multi-core capabilities, supported by AVX and AVX-512F instruction sets. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for CPU-burstable workloads with low memory requirements. It is best suited for lightweight multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and development environments.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m1.2xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance with 8 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 2.5 GHz CPU, offering cost-effective performance for variable, low-memory workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m1.2xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU on a KVM hypervisor.
tagline: A burstable, multi-core x86_64 compute resource designed for variable workloads requiring minimal memory overhead and cost-effective scaling.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor
Burstable CPU allocation model for handling intermittent traffic spikes
Average multi-core performance in stress-ng and Passmark benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for lightweight multi-threaded applications and development environments
